name: Akon&Ankyle
description: Greek Javelin with throwing strap >3 can be held in one hand

name: Aspis
description: Hoplite shield

name: Bagh Nakh
description: Fighting claws which are mounted over the user’s own hands.

name: Battle Net
description: Saparu. Nets are used to entrap and keep prisoners and slaves. They are large and difficult to use in combat, but can be effective when thrown from a wall onto a scaling ladder, or from a chariot at fleeing enemies.

name: Belt/sash chain
description: Disguised weighted chain

name: Bidentis
description: An oddly weighted spear made from the paired fangs of a sabre-tooth tiger set at right angles to the shaft. It is intended to be swept down upon the head or back of an opponent to embed the fangs.

name: Bo staff
description: Quarterstaff made from oak or hardwood roughly 1.8 m in length.

name: Bokken
description: Practice samurai sword made of oak

name: Broad Axe 1H
description: Hassinu. The broad axe has a wide blade set into notches in the haft. It sees use as a weapon by city garrisons and gatekeepers; it is useful for slicing ropes and sweeping besiegers off ladders... And it looks imposing.

name: Broad Axe 2H
description: Hassinu. The broad axe has a wide blade set into notches in the haft. It sees use as a weapon by city garrisons and gatekeepers; it is useful for slicing ropes and sweeping besiegers off ladders... And it looks imposing.

name: Caetra
description: Small Round Shield

name: Cakera
description: A type of chakram, appearing as a circular ring of copper intended for throwing, whose outer edge is razor sharp with protruding, oddly curved spikes.

name: Chigriki
description: Japanese flail weapon. It consists of a solid or hollow wood (sometimes bamboo) or iron staff with an iron weight and chain on the end, sometimes retractable. The chigiriki is a more aggressive variation of the parrying weapon kusarigama. It can be used to strike or entangle the opponent as well as to parry his blows and to capture or incapacitate an opponents weapon.\n\nThe stick can be as long as the wielder's forearm or longer, while the length of the chain could also be of various lengths. The iron weight could have spikes and its shape could be round or be multi sided.

name: Chimalli
description: A large circular shield, formed from a range of different materials and decorated with coloured feathers. The basic version is a frame of fire-hardened wooden rods, covered with woven cloth or dinosaur hide; whilst others can be formed from turtle shells. The High Folk sometimes use copper to sheathe their shields.

name: Chokin
description: These are small throwing weapons made either of flint or obsidian, which can impale. The dragonewt may throw up to three chokin or four samarin at the same time during a single Combat Action.

name: Comb
description: Metal hair comb with wicked teeth

name: Cosh
description: Weighted, flexible weapon

name: Cuauhololli
description: The Cuahololli was a mace made of wood with a ball at the end.

name: Dagger Cane
description: Stiletto concealed in the hilt of a cane

name: Dai-kyu
description: Dai-kyu is asymmetrical bow and can be up to 2.5m long and can be used from saddle.  Materials are laminated bamboo and wood with rattan twines

name: Daichui
description: Twin skulls, carved from stone or crystal or real skulls plated in copper, which are connected by a copper chain. Although intended to be a flexible bashing weapon, the types of skulls utilised in its construction can offer the Bleed effect if their dentition is sufficiently nasty or the chance of igniting an opponent if a skull is filled with a flammable fluid. Due to its length one end can entangle a foe, whilst still leaving the other free to continue battering.

name: Dao
description: Chinese 1 edged sabre

name: Dory
description: The dory was about 2 to 3 m (6 ft 7 in to 9 ft 10 in) in length and had a handle with a diameter of 5 cm (two inches) made of wood, either cornel or ash weighing 2 to 4 lb (0.91 to 1.81 kg). The flat leaf-shaped spearhead was composed of iron and its weight was counterbalanced by an iron butt-spike.[2][3][4] (cf Sarissa)\nThe rear of the spear was capped with a spike called a sauroter (Greek: σαυρωτήρ), Greek for "lizard killer". It functionally served as a counter-weight to give balance. This spike had several uses. It could be used to stand the spear up or used as a secondary weapon if the spearhead was broken off.[5] If the shaft of the dory was broken or if the iron point was lost, the remaining portion could still function.[6] Though its combat range would be reduced, the dory\'s complete length would have lessened the chance of a single break rendering it ineffective. Additionally, any enemies that had fallen could be dispatched by the warriors marching over them in the back ranks of the phalanx who were holding their spears in a vertical position.[5] (Wikipedia)

name: Eku
description: An oar-spear, quite literally a thrusting spear with a broadened end making it useful for paddling small rafts or canoes. The paddle can substitute as a club, permitting access to the Stun Location effect.

name: Espasa
description: Sword

name: Falcata
description: Short sword

name: Falchion
description: Short, heavy blade with a single edge.

name: Fingertip Needles
description: Thimble-like finger coverings which possess a hypoder- mic needle that emerges from its tip. The needle is con- nected to a small reservoir of poison, permitting the user to select precisely which venom to utilise on a victim, provided they wear more than one of these items. Learning to use Finger Needles requires tuition in the Eight Jab Doom combat style.

name: Fist
description: *) SIZ 1-10 1d3. SIZ 11-20 damage is 1d4

name: Gada
description: A type of stone mace whose head is carved into that of a demon, monster or skull depending on the maker.

name: Gami
description: A gami is a three-pronged swordcatcher made of dragonbone, exceptionally effective at parrying

name: Airofai
description: The design of the airofai means that maximum damage is always inflicted when the weapon is withdrawn. The airofai is also coated with stingray venom, which is said to cause such pain that even a god would wish for death. Airofai carry a single dose of the venom.

name: Gladius
description: Stabbing sword

name: Glaive
description: A glaive (or glave) is a polearm, consisting of a single-edged blade on the end of a pole. Typically, the blade is around 45 centimetres (18 in) long, on the end of a pole 2 metres (7 ft) long, and the blade is affixed in a socket-shaft configuration similar to an axe head, rather than having a tang like a sword or naginata. Occasionally, glaive blades were created with a small hook on the reverse side to better catch riders. Such blades are called glaive-guisarmes.

name: Glaive/Rhomphaia
description: A glaive (or glave) is a polearm, consisting of a single-edged blade on the end of a pole. Typically, the blade is around 45 centimetres (18 in) long, on the end of a pole 2 metres (7 ft) long, and the blade is affixed in a socket-shaft configuration similar to an axe head, rather than having a tang like a sword or naginata. Occasionally, glaive blades were created with a small hook on the reverse side to better catch riders. Such blades are called glaive-guisarmes.

name: Grenado
description: 1d8+1 to 1d3 locations. In addition any person within 1 metre of the bomb takes 1d6+1 damage to 1d2 adjacent locations.

name: Han Kyu
description: A shorter version of dai-kyu - the asymmetric bow

name: Hanbo
description: 1 meters wooden staff

name: Hasta
description: Spear

name: Hatpin
description: Stiletto disguised as a hatpin or hairpin

name: Hook gun
description: Note MC rules on Firearms mishaps, Size and distance modifications, Discharging and reloading(pg 73)

name: Iron Rimed Oval Shield (Scutum) - Early Republic
Iron Rimed Oval Shield (Scutum) - Mid Republic
Ixcheltu
description: A thorn- or shark tooth-embedded rope which is used rather like a whip, but can be applied as a garrotte in a stealth situation. Due to the barbs the rope can inflict serious bleeding.

name: Jo staff
description: Jo staff is made of oak or hardwood. It is around 1.2m in length. Jojutsu is faster than longer Bo.

name: Katar
description: Punch daggers, with a wide triangular blade mounted on a frame so that the point extends before the knuckles. They are normally made from copper and bear inscriptions as to the brotherhood to which they belongs.

name: Khopesh
description: A strange and fabulous sword, the khopesh has a sickle- like blade with its outside curve bearing the edge of the weapon. The shape of the weapon makes for heavier blows but at the expense of the blade’s speed.

name: Iron Rimed Rectangular Shield - Late Republic
Klanth 1H
description: A klanth is a weapon constructed of flint blades set into a wooden haft. It is normally used one handed by dragonewts of sufficient strength, two handed by others

name: Klanth 2H
description: A klanth is a weapon constructed of flint blades set into a wooden haft. It is normally used one handed by dragonewts of sufficient strength, two handed by others

name: Koipahoa
description: A type of pseudo-axe which is formed from a tusk or giant tooth attached crosswise to a short haft.

name: Kontos
description: a long cavalry lance about 3.6 to 4.2 meters long with a sword-like blade and a butt spike. It is heavier than a lance with a thicker shaft, and less likely to break.

name: Kopis
description: Curved sword

name: Korff
description: A slightly-curved dragonbone sword that appears to be made of ivory. Dragonewts will go to any length to recover a lost korff.

name: Kpinga
description: Triple-bladed throwing knife, shaped so that the target is hit by one or more of the razor sharp edges, even rolling over the edge of a shield to strike the wielder behind it.

name: Kusari
description: length of chain weighted at both ends

name: Lancet
description: long and thin sharp spines made entirely of a single sea-tree splinter. They are used two-handed,

name: Lancet Ranged
description: long and thin sharp spines made entirely of a single sea-tree splinter. They are used two-handed,

name: Large Handgonne
description: Note MC rules on Firearms mishaps, Size and distance modifications, Discharging and reloading(pg 73)

name: Kratosi
description: An ovoid wicker shield with thorns and sharks’ teeth set into its face, so that striking weapons, or even an enemy shield, can get caught on its surface.

name: Large Rhomphaia
comment: Rhomphaias were weapons with a straight or slightly curved single-edged\xa0bladeattached to a pole, which in most cases was considerably shorter than the blade.
description: Rhomphaias were weapons with a straight or slightly curved single-edged\xa0bladeattached to a pole, which in most cases was considerably shorter than the blade.  A glaive (or glave) is a polearm, consisting of a single-edged blade on the end of a pole. Typically, the blade is around 45 centimetres (18 in) long, on the end of a pole 2 metres (7 ft) long, and the blade is affixed in a socket-shaft configuration similar to an axe head, rather than having a tang like a sword or naginata. Occasionally, glaive blades were created with a small hook on the reverse side to better catch riders. Such blades are called glaive-guisarmes.

name: Maduvu
description: A wavy-bladed dagger carved from the spiral horn of some monstrous beast. Its purpose is more of an assassination weapon, since the horn blade tends to break off in the wound, preventing healing from occurring until it is surgically removed. In addition the porous horn is an ideal method of injecting poisons into the victim.

name: Main Gauche
description: Main Gauche is a left handed dagger that is used to guard when fencing.

name: Makhaira
description: Curved cavalry sword. The makhaira is a type of Ancient Greek bladed weapon, generally a large knife or sword with a single cutting edge. Used often by cavalry

name: Manglabia
description: A whip made of flexible bullhide, 1.5 m long.

name: Maquahuitl 1H
description: Swords made from exceptionally tough hardwood, with edges inset with razor-sharp flakes of obsidian. They tend to be more fragile than metal equivalents, but inflict very nasty wounds. Maquahuitl come in single-handed and double-handed versions.

name: Maquahuitl 2H
description: Swords made from exceptionally tough hardwood, with edges inset with razor-sharp flakes of obsidian. They tend to be more fragile than metal equivalents, but inflict very nasty wounds. Maquahuitl come in single-handed and double-handed versions.

name: Masakari
description: Axe with a very large head with a very\xa0convex\xa0cutting edge and a large scroll-shaped\xa0peen\xa0opposite it. It has a sheath covering only the edge of the blade. The entire length is nearly six feet

name: MB Club
description: Napassu. A club is simply an impromptu bludgeoning instrument – a stout stick or a bone. Cudgels also fall under this category. MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Javelin (Bronze)
description: MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Javelin (sharpened stake)
description: MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Knife (Bronze)
description: Patarru. MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Knife (Stone)
description: MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Mace
description: Masgasu. MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Self Bow
description: MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Sickle Sword
description: Pastu. The sickle-sword is cast as a single piece of bronze, making it strong and durable. The handle is bound with cloth or leather. Sickle-swords contain more bronze per pound than any other type of weapon, so they are extremely expensive.Their use is limited to kings, favoured military officers, or the very wealthiest of citizens. MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Sling
description: MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Spear (Bronze)
description: Sukurru. The spear is by far the most common military weapon of the period. Spears are between 2.5 and 4 m long and used two- handed, either in formation when on the ground or to reach enemies on siege towers or ladders from the top of the wall. They have copper or copper-alloy heads and are attached to the haft by a shoe-socket. Older tanged-style heads are still in use here and there but are more prone to breakage.MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Spear (Stone)
description: Sukurru. The spear is by far the most common military weapon of the period. Spears are between 2.5 and 4 m long and used two- handed, either in formation when on the ground or to reach enemies on siege towers or ladders from the top of the wall. They have copper or copper-alloy heads and are attached to the haft by a shoe-socket. Older tanged-style heads are still in use here and there but are more prone to breakage.MB means Mythic Babylon

name: MB Whip
description: Qinazzu. Whips are made from braided hide, and mainly used to goad animals or intimidate (or trip up) prisoners.MB means Mythic Babylon

name: Meilichai
description: Leather fingerless gloves worn by pankratiasts (see page 21). If sewn with bronze beads, increases the damage of unarmed attacks to 1d4.

name: Military Flail
description: Short handle attached to a chain, at the end of which is a metal ball.

name: Naginata
description: A naginata consists of a wooden or metal pole with a curved single-edged blade on the end; Naginata often have a round handguard (tsuba) between the blade and shaft, when mounted in a koshirae. The 30 cm to 60 cm long naginata blade. The shaft ranges from 120 cm to 240 cm in length and is oval shaped. The end of the shaft has a heavy metal end cap (ishizuki or hirumaki). When not in use the blade would be covered with a wooden sheath

name: Ninja-to 1H
description: The ninjatō is  a short sword, often portrayed as having a straight blade with a square guard. Usually of a length "less than 60 cm", the rest of the sword is comparatively "thick, heavy and straight".

name: Ninja-to 2H
description: The ninjatō is  a short sword, often portrayed as having a straight blade with a square guard. Usually of a length "less than 60 cm", the rest of the sword is comparatively "thick, heavy and straight".

name: No-Dachi
description: The no-dachi greatsword is usually carried at your back and can be as long as two metres

name: Ono
description: Axe with a very large head with a very\xa0convex\xa0cutting edge and a large scroll-shaped\xa0peen\xa0opposite it. It has a sheath covering only the edge of the blade. The entire length is nearly six feet

name: Mubuchae
description: A fan with reinforced spars, so that it is able to parry incoming attacks and even cover a limited number of Hit Locations as if it were a shield. The fan’s upper edge when opened is able to inflict cutting attacks

name: Parashu
description: Wooden axe embedded with sharks’ teeth. Most are shaped with exotic bends in the haft, or weirdly shaped heads.

name: Orlanthi Scutum Shield
description: rectangular, semi-cylindrical shield

name: Patu
description: Stone clubs carved into a flat, ovoid shape. These are usually made of smoothed obsidian, but a few priceless examples are carved from Jadestone. Patu faces often depict unusual designs, dependent upon the Brotherhood or Tribe it originated from.

name: Parma
description: Small Wooden Round Shield

name: Pavise
description: A pavise is a large shield used for cover that can be stood on the ground with an attached spike or worn on the back. Treat as a Scutum

name: Pelorian Aspis
description: Hoplite shield

name: Pick
description: Used by eastern cavalry

name: Piercing Axe
description: Zhatu. Some piercing Axes are double ended

name: Pike
description: a long two-handed thrusting spear between 3 and 6 meters long.

name: Pike/Sarissa
description: a long two-handed thrusting spear between 3 and 6 meters long.

name: Ptsath 1H
description: A spear by any other name, it has a carefully napped flint or obsidian head, each one uniquely shaped with razor sharp spurs and flanges, for that particular dragonewt. When used to impale a victim the fragile, decorative barbs tend to break off, causing a further 1d3 points of damage per round of using/flexing the location even after the Ptsath is withdrawn, until the fragments are removed by surgery.

name: Ptsath 2H
description: A spear by any other name, it has a carefully napped flint or obsidian head, each one uniquely shaped with razor sharp spurs and flanges, for that particular dragonewt. When used to impale a victim the fragile, decorative barbs tend to break off, causing a further 1d3 points of damage per round of using/flexing the location even after the Ptsath is withdrawn, until the fragments are removed by surgery.

name: Qian
description: Chinese double edged sword

name: Roman Kopis
description: Curved sword

name: Roman Kopis
description: Hacking sword

name: Roman Xiphos
description: Short sword

name: Sai dagger
description: Sai dagger is a blunted, prong shaped metal baton, with two curved prongs (yoku) projecting from the handle

name: Samarin
description: These are small throwing weapons made either of flint or obsidian, which can impale. The dragonewt may throw up to three chokin or four samarin at the same time during a single Combat Action.

name: Sarissa
description: a long two-handed thrusting spear between 3 and 6 meters long.

name: Reed Shield
description: Ranged Parry ONLY

name: Rotella
description: Larger than the civilian buckler, small enough to be quickly wielded in a fast aggressive style. (Wikipedia)

name: Sectional Staff
description: three sticks linked by cord or chain.

name: Shinobi-kyu
description: A shorter version of dai-kyu - the asymmetric bow. Collapsible short bow of Ninja. Cannot be bought

name: Small Handgonne
description: Leather fingerless gloves worn by pankratiasts (see page 21). If sewn with bronze beads, increases the damage of unarmed attacks to 1d4.

name: Small Rhomphaia
comment: Rhomphaias were weapons with a straight or slightly curved single-edged\xa0bladeattached to a pole, which in most cases was considerably shorter than the blade.
description: Rhomphaias were weapons with a straight or slightly curved single-edged\xa0bladeattached to a pole, which in most cases was considerably shorter than the blade. . A glaive (or glave) is a polearm, consisting of a single-edged blade on the end of a pole. Typically, the blade is around 45 centimetres (18 in) long, on the end of a pole 2 metres (7 ft) long, and the blade is affixed in a socket-shaft configuration similar to an axe head, rather than having a tang like a sword or naginata. Occasionally, glaive blades were created with a small hook on the reverse side to better catch riders. Such blades are called glaive-guisarmes.

name: Scutum Shield
description: rectangular, semi-cylindrical shield

name: Solenarion
description: A hollow tube held in the place of an arrow on a bow, through which an archer can launch small darts or mues (“mice”). The advantage is that these darts may be used in very close quarters at a rapid rate of fire. Treat as a dart but inflicting 1d6 damage. The solenarion can also fire several darts at a time, acquiring the Scatter Weapon trait

name: Spatha
description: Cavalry sword

name: Staff of Chastisement
description: Consuming Annihilation spell. Their precise effects and shaping varies from staff to staff, but it costs 1 or more Magic Points to acti-vate the staff depending on what parameters the matrix has (divide 10 shaping points between Magnitude, Range and Targets). For the next few minutes, the staff can be used to launch a 1d10 damage attack on the wielder’s turn (ignoring worn armour). Blasts must be aimed with a Combat Style that includes this magical item (which some militant High Folk brotherhoods teach) and the aiming roll is resisted in an opposed roll as per normal to overcome the spell.

name: Stiletto
description: Small dagger for stabbing, not cutting

name: Sword Cane
description: Rapier concealed in the hilt of a cane

name: Tachi
description: Katana type sword with cutting-edge down. Average cutting edge length (nagasa) of 70–80 cm  and compared to a katana was generally lighter in proportion to its length, had a greater taper from hilt to point, was more curved and had a smaller point area

name: Taiaha
description: Dinosaur bone staff with a stabbing point at one end and a flattened blade at the other, used for bashing rather than cutting. The staff is generally decorated with collars or tufts of feathers for decoration.

name: Taibumi
description: Ninja weapon .Short bow that can be folded to walking stick

name: Tanto
description: The blade is single or double edged with a length between 15 and 30cm (1 Japanese shaku). The tantō was designed primarily as a stabbing weapon, but the edge can be used for slashing as well.

name: Tepoztopilli
description: A two handed cross between a spear and a halberd, with an edge formed from obsidian flakes similar to Maquahuitl

name: Tetsubo
description: Metal staff 120+cm long. Weighs 6kg+

name: Tamking Shield
description: A long, narrow spike-edged shield made of woven rattan, that whilst less protective offers a greater deal of offense, since its edge can be used to slash, being embedded with sharks’ teeth.

name: Tonfa
description: It consists of a stick with a perpendicular handle attached a third of the way down the length of the stick, and is about 15–20 inches (380–510 mm) long. It was traditionally made from red or white oak and wielded in pairs

name: Trishula
description: Stone headed tridents with blades which are capable of penetrating copper or bronze armour. Most are ground in such a way that the edges are serrated with rearwards pointing barbs making them vicious when withdrawn from an impaling wound. They can be used one or two handed.

name: Trishula
description: Stone headed tridents with blades which are capable of penetrating copper or bronze armour. Most are ground in such a way that the edges are serrated with rearwards pointing barbs making them vicious when withdrawn from an impaling wound. They can be used one or two handed.

name: Tulwar
description: Heavy two-handed scimitar

name: Tzath
description: Throwing javelins which have similarly shaped flint or obsidian heads as Ptsath, including the nasty ability of breaking off in the wound.

name: Urumi
description: The coiling blade, literally a razor-edged sword made of copper thin enough to be coiled about the waist or forearms. Unfortunately its portability and ability to be concealed make the weapon vulnerable to being broken.

name: Utuma
description: This weapon is the dragonewt version of a shortsword. Usually, it is a reinforced flint blade, but a very rare utuma is made from dragon bone.

name: Vajramusti
description: Knuckledusters with stabbing spikes protruding from either end.

name: Targe
description: a concave shield fitted with enarmes on the inside, one adjustable by a buckle, to be attached to the forearm, and the other fixed as a grip for the left hand. (wikipedia)

name: Wahaika
description: A wooden or dinosaur bone club, similar to a Patu except that it has a notch on one side used to capture an opponent’s weapon.

name: Wakizashi
description: Shortsword Katana, usually a backup weapon for samurai

name: Weighted Cane
description: Reinforced, weighted cane

name: Weighted Gloves
description: Gloves with hidden weigths

name: Thuroes
description: A\xa0thyreos\xa0(Ancient Greek:\xa0θυρεός) was a large oval\xa0shield\xa0which was commonly used in\xa0Hellenistic\xa0armies\xa0

name: Xiphidion
description: Stabbing sword

name: Xiphos
description: The\xa0xiphos\xa0(Ancient Greek:\xa0ξίφος\xa0[ksípʰos]; plural\xa0xiphe,\xa0Ancient Greek:\xa0ξίφη\xa0[ksípʰɛː])[1]\xa0is a double-edged, one-handed\xa0Iron Age straight shortsword\xa0used by the\xa0ancient Greeks. It was a secondary battlefield weapon for the Greek armies after the\xa0dory\xa0or\xa0javelin. The classic\xa0blade\xa0was generally about 45–60\xa0cm (18–24\xa0in) long, although the Spartans supposedly preferred to use blades as short as 30\xa0cm (12\xa0in) around the era of the\xa0Greco-Persian Wars.\xa0

name: Xuele
description: Xuele is a curved short blade with ritual significance used by Spartans

name: Xyston
description: Spear or lance.  It measured about 3.5–4.25 meters (11.5–13.9 ft) long and was probably held by the cavalryman with both hands. It had a wooden shaft and a spear-point at both ends.

name: Yari
description: Eastern longspear.Yari has a straight blade that could be anywhere from several centimeters to 3 feet or more in length

name: Yari-nage
description: The shortened, javelin-like version of the yari

name: Yawara
description: small object held in one hand to amplify the effect of punch

name: Zagaie
description: A\xa0weapon\xa0made in the form of a long dart,

name: Patarru
description: Patarru

name: Polearm (Bill)
description: A\xa0bill\xa0is a class of agricultural implement used for trimming tree limbs, which was often repurposed for use as an infantry\xa0polearm. (Wikipedia)

name: Polearm (Halberd)
description: The halberd consists of an axe blade topped with a spike mounted on a long shaft. It always has a hook or thorn on the back side of the axe blade for grappling mounted combatants. (Wikipedia)

name: Gaff
description: A gaff hook is a long pole with a hook on one end for spearing fish

name: Polearm (Partisan)
description: t consisted of a spearhead mounted on a long shaft, usually wooden, with protrusions on the sides which aided in parrying sword thrusts. (Wikipedia)

name: Polearm (Glaive)
description: A glaive is a polearm consisting of a single-edged tapering blade similar in shape to a modern kitchen knife on the end of a pole. The blade was around 18 inches (46 cm) long, on the end of a pole 6 or 7 feet (180 or 210 centimetres) long (Wikipedia)

name: Shillelagh
description: A shillelagh is a wooden walking stick and club or cudgel, typically made from a stout knotty blackthorn stick with a large knob at the top. (Wikipedia)

name: Akinake
description: And the so-called sicarii, which were a type of bandit, were at that time reaching their greatest number, making use of small swords, which were like the Persian acinaces in respect to their size, but curved like the Roman sica, which is where these bandits got their name.

name: Wooden Oval Shield (Aspis)
description: Hoplite shield

name: 2H Khopesh
description: A strange and fabulous sword, the khopesh has a sickle- like blade with its outside curve bearing the edge of the weapon. The shape of the weapon makes for heavier blows but at the expense of the blade’s speed.

name: apa'apai War Club
description: coconut-stalk club (apa'apai in Tonga, uatongi in Samoa), named for its resemblance to a growing coconut palm. The club is 0.75-1 metre long with a diamond-shaped cross section.\nIt flares towards the far end and is cut off square, usually with a concave divot at the end.

name: kinikini War Club
description: exclusive preserve of a high- ranking chief and cannot be used by others. A similar weapon is called a u'u, here the blade bears the face of the god of war. The wide blades can be used as a shield to block incoming missile weapons.

name: Kotiate - War Club
description: flat with an oval or rectangular blade; two T-shaped notches are carved into each long edge. It is used close to the body to defend against incoming attacks.

name: Sharktooth War Club
description: coconut-stalk club (apa'apai in Tonga, uatongi in Samoa), named for its resemblance to a growing coconut palm. The club is 0.75-1 metre long with a diamond-shaped cross section.\nIt flares towards the far end and is cut off square, usually with a concave divot at the end.

name: Taiaha Pole Club
description: When used to Damage Weapon Special Effect, roll damage twice and take the highest

name: Talavalu (War Club)
description: Coconut-stalk club named for its resemblance to a growing coconut palm. The club is 0.75-1 metre long with a diamond-shaped cross section. It has eight pyramidal spikes, four on either side, projecting from the central body. \nIt flares towards the far end and is cut off square, usually with a concave divot at the end.

name: u'u War Club
description: exclusive preserve of a high- ranking chief and cannot be used by others. A similar weapon is called a u'u, here the blade bears the face of the god of war. The wide blades can be used as a shield to block incoming missile weapons.

name: uatongi War Club
description: coconut-stalk club (apa'apai in Tonga, uatongi in Samoa), named for its resemblance to a growing coconut palm. The club is 0.75-1 metre long with a diamond-shaped cross section.\nIt flares towards the far end and is cut off square, usually with a concave divot at the end.

name: ulufuamiti (War Club)
description: curved with a flared blade ending in a sharp spike which can be used like a spear.

name: Wahaika - Hand Club
description: looks like a mere but has a wide semi-circular notch lined with teeth; this is used to trap an opponent's weapon.

name: War Club
description: coconut-stalk club (apa'apai in Tonga, uatongi in Samoa), named for its resemblance to a growing coconut palm. The club is 0.75-1 metre long with a diamond-shaped cross section.\nIt flares towards the far end and is cut off square, usually with a concave divot at the end.

name: Machete
description: Short, heavy blade with a single edge.
